Given $n\in\mathbb{N}$ , let $a_{n}$ be the number of distinct ways the particle has to go from $A_{0}(0,0)\mathrm{~t o~}A_{n}(n,n)$ , without ever touching a point $(x,y)$ situated above the bisector of odd quadrants (i.e., one such point for which $y>x)$ . In this respect, do the following items:
(a) Let $A_{k}(k,k)$ ,with $0\leq k<n$ . Prove that there are exactly $a_{k}a_{n-1-k}$ distinct trajectories for the particle in which $A_{k}$ is the last point (before $A_{n})$ on the line $y=x$
(b)Conclude that $\begin{array}{r}{a_{n}=\sum_{k=0}^{n-1}a_{k}a_{n-1-k}}\end{array}$ and, hence, that $a_{n}=C_{n}{\mathrm{~f o r~}}n\geq1$ where $C_{n}$ is the n-th Catalan number.
For the coming problem, the reader may find it convenient to read again the paragraph that precedes Example 1.15.

mid n\ & rac{1}{6} inom{n+2}{2}+ rac{1}{4}(n+1)+ rac{1}{3},\mathrm{i f}2

3. Given k,$m\in\mathbb{N}$ ,use generating functions to compute the number of integer solutions of the equation $a_{1}+a_{2}+\cdots+a_{k}=m$ ,such that $a_{i}\geq1\;{\mathrm{f o r}}\;1\leq i\leq k$ 4.

Given $n\in\mathbb{N}$ , let $a_{n}$ be the number of distinct ways the particle has to go from $A_{0}(0,0)\mathrm{~t o~}A_{n}(n,n)$ , without ever touching a point $(x,y)$ situated above the bisector of odd quadrants (i.e., one such point for which $y>x)$ . In this respect, do the following items:

(a) Let $A_{k}(k,k)$ ,with $0\leq k<n$ . Prove that there are exactly $a_{k}a_{n-1-k}$ distinct trajectories for the particle in which $A_{k}$ is the last point (before $A_{n})$ on the line $y=x$ 



(b)Conclude that $\begin{array}{r}{a_{n}=\sum_{k=0}^{n-1}a_{k}a_{n-1-k}}\end{array}$ and, hence, that $a_{n}=C_{n}{\mathrm{~f o r~}}n\geq1$ where $C_{n}$ is the n-th Catalan number.



For the coming problem, the reader may find it convenient to read again the paragraph that precedes Example 1.15.



6. For $n\in\mathbb{N}$ ,we let $a_{n}$ denote the number of partitions of n in natural summands,none of which exceeds 3. The purposeof this problem is to compute $a_{n}$ as a function of $n$ , ad tohisd om 

(a) Show that, for $|x|<1$ , one has 

$$\sum_{n\geq1}a_{n}x^{n}={\frac{1}{(1-x)(1-x^{2})(1-x^{3})}}.$$

(b) Find a, , c,$d\in\mathbb{R}$ .for which 

$$\frac{1}{(1-x)(1-x^{2})(1-x^{3})}=\frac{a}{(1-x)^{3}}+\frac{b}{(1-x)^{2}}+\frac{c}{1-x^{2}}+\frac{d}{1-x^{3}}.$$

(c)  Conclude that 

$$a_{n}=\left\{\begin{aligned}&\frac{1}{6}\binom{n+2}{2}+\frac{1}{4}(n+1)+\frac{7}{12},\mathrm{i f}6\mid n\\ &\frac{1}{6}\binom{n+2}{2}+\frac{1}{4}(n+1)+\frac{1}{4},\mathrm{i f}2\mid n\mathrm{b u t}3\nmid n\\ &\frac{1}{6}\binom{n+2}{2}+\frac{1}{4}(n+1)+\frac{1}{3},\mathrm{i f}2\nmid n\mathrm{b u t}3\mid n\\ &\frac{1}{6}\binom{n+2}{2}+\frac{1}{4}(n+1),\mathrm{i f}2,3\nmid n\end{aligned}\right..$$
